Block Scoring

Open Interactive Demo
Watch blocks scored by CMS frequency and HLL cardinality with early termination
→

Overview

After the pruning stages (bloom filter, KLL, CMS) eliminate blocks that cannot contain matching spans, the remaining candidate blocks are scored and sorted before any I/O is issued. Higher-scored blocks are fetched first, enabling early termination when a result limit is reached.

Scoring Formula

score = sum(CMS_frequency) / max(HLL_cardinality, 1)
  • CMS_frequency — how many times the queried value appears in this block (Count-Min Sketch estimate)
  • HLL_cardinality — how many distinct values exist for that column in this block (HyperLogLog estimate)
  • High frequency + low cardinality = high selectivity = block is likely to have many matches

Sort Order

Blocks are sorted by:

  1. Timestamp (primary) — blocks within the query time window, in time order
  2. Score descending (secondary) — within the same time bucket, higher-scoring blocks first

Implementation: queryplanner/planner.go:setToSortedByScore

Early Termination

If the query has a result limit (e.g. limit=10), blockpack stops fetching blocks once the limit is satisfied. Lower-scored blocks that were never fetched cost zero I/O. For highly selective queries (e.g. a specific trace ID or rare service), often only 1-2 blocks are read regardless of how many passed the pruning stages.

Example

Using the query { span.service.name = "auth" && duration > 100ms } against 5 blocks after pruning leaves blk-001 and blk-003:

BlockCMS("auth")HLL(service.name)ScoreFetch order
blk-0034201420.01st
blk-0013122156.02nd

With limit=10: blk-003 returns 7 spans, blk-001 returns 3 spans → total 10, stop. blk-005 is never fetched.

Source

FilePurpose
internal/modules/queryplanner/scoring.goScore formula implementation
internal/modules/queryplanner/planner.gosetToSortedByScore — sorts candidates
internal/modules/executor/stream.goEarly termination on result limit

Related

  • TopK — top-K values contribute to CMS frequency
  • HyperLogLog — cardinality denominator
  • Count-Min Sketch — frequency numerator
